title: Mild to Moderate Asthma Attack
immediate_steps: Help the person sit comfortably upright
Reassure them and keep them calm
Help them use their reliever inhaler (usually blue), ideally with a spacer: 1 puff every 30-60 seconds, up to 10 puffs
If symptoms improve, continue to monitor them
avoid: Do not lay the person down
Do not expose them to known triggers (smoke, cold air)

title: Severe Asthma Attack
symptoms: Silent chest (no wheezing because air is not moving)
immediate_steps: Help the person sit up and lean slightly forward
Continue helping them use their reliever inhaler (1 puff every 30-60 seconds, up to 10 puffs, and repeat after 15 minutes if help hasn't arrived)
Stay calm and keep the person as calm as possible
avoid: Do not wait to call an ambulance if the inhaler is not working
Do not force them to lie flat
